Apply larger tilt correction factor initially. | It got messed up by the gyro filtering addition. Without the fix, if you dock the phone with the headset facing down you will observe very very slow tilt correction every time.
Camera2 Sample: force finish the activity on pause as a workaround; | otherwise the sample doesn't work properly on resume.
Prevent a loading texture being rendered in a bad/green state | This covers all cases of textures and shaders comphrensively in addition to previous checkin.

Fix final frame in animation | If an animation stops at the last frame, it might be warpped to the first frame.This can be fixed by adding a small value to the duration (1e-6).The common code in GVRSkinningController and GVRNodeAnimationController is pulledinto a base class, and the fixed is applied on that.Rename some variables to distinguish time vs ticks in the animation controller(s).
Cubemap texture jni error fixed. |  The cubemap sample was crashing immediately after the splash screen was dismissed because of it.- applied similar change to other affected textures
Draw a black border for each eye texture per Oculus requirement | This prevents artifacts along the edge of each eye.
Fix rendering objects for one eye only | Skip execution only if render masks do not match.This fixes regression introduced by commit 59920e2.

Adding a default text color to TextViewSceneObject | Adding a default text color to TextViewSceneObject.  That seems to fixit for the gvr-remote-scripting sample anyway.GearVRf-DCO-1.0-Signed-off-by: Tom Flynntom.flynn@samsung.com
Fixed material transparency in phong shader | The Phong shader was ignoring the alpha in the diffuse color of thematerial. Now it is correctly applying it.

fix exception in GVRScene.clear, check max light count | The exception for adding an object twice was triggered by GVRScene.clear(camera rig owner was parented twice).The scene now checks for maximum light number and wont generateduplicate light  names.GearVRf-DCO-1.0-Signed-off-by: nola.donato@samsung.com Nola Donato

Fixed picking bug (fixes issue #800) | The wrong matrix was being used for transforming the picking ray causingit to pick the wrong thing.Now the picker actually looks at the object it is attached to and getsits position and orientation from that. I am doing a pull request for these demos.Removed loggin
Fix invalidateFrameBuffer() for multisample | After multisample resolve, invalidateFrameBuffer() was discarding resolved color buffer instead of original multisample framebuffer.

Fix: make GVRLineSceneObject render properly | Fixes GVRLineSceneObject so it can render properly in scene.

fix multiview rendering for render-mask | this fixes multiview rendering if one of the eye rendering is disabled. Changed uniform locations from GLuint to GLint. Fix for no final audio effect when a UI element vanishes. | When a UI element vanishes upon activation, it doesn't get a chance to play its completion audio effect. This stores the completion audio effect external to the UI element, so it can be activated automatically when the UI element id stops being active.

While these elements are not interactive, and aren't actually enabled/disabled exactly, it is generally the case that devs expect these elements to reflect the current enabled/disabled status.

Add `UI.SetElementColor` and `UI.SetElementSound`, plus fixes. #793 | Also fixes a few other things- UI.SetElementVisual now properly sends minimum size down to C++.- UI.Toggle properly uses the visual from UIVisual.Toggle instead of button.- Progress bars are no longer permanently focused.

fix: check if the secondary display active when only one is present | Continuing off #816, the view count is 1 when it both is & isn't recording. I stepped through the code and found that it doesn't check if only one secondary display is active. For each frame, when it's inactive, we don't try to locate the device. When it's active, the swapchain gets updated! I can confirm this works for both active/inactive scenarios. Such a small change for the copyright header but I put that in there too :D

Handle FaceUser from center | Previously, orientation was determined using the centerline of the handle, but with the Y axis set where the user gripped. This was helpful when UI was very large and the user needed a way to shift the UI around and look at specific parts, but was not always seen as desirable behavior when windows were sized appropriately.To me, while this does feel more consisten, it also feels a little clumsy in comparison for large panels. This may be more an indication the UI is

Skybox fixes. | Some strange Android behavior related to the skybox, Quest was recently or occasionally displaying as black? Not sure when this started happening, or if it actually is happening consistently, maybe some recent driver thing?Simplified a bit of shader code workarounds as well. Previously `transpose` was not working with either SK's transpiler or the Android driver's shader compiler. Seems to be fine now though. Also shifted depth back to a full 1, and swapped the depth test to less
Fix for file picker disabling UI. | Fixed some push/pop enabled issues in the file picker. Usage pattern was not right.

Fix for hand mesh corrupting when switching between the hand mesh EXT. | Switching between StereoKit's fallback hand mesh and the hand mesh extension would cause the mesh to get corrupted! This fixes that issue, and adds a window that allows you to inspect all available controller data.

Interaction improvements | - Fixed direct interaction pinches using the aim ready states.- Fixed incorrect visual focus indicator on elements that were merely focus candidates, rather than successfully focused elements.- Fixes far rays interacting with all overlapping elements rather than nearest.- Make far rays vanish sooner at arm's reach.- Move hand rays visually further forward.- More reactive ray visual behavior.- Tuned down ray snapping.
Fix aura fade | Grab aura was not fading correctly, was just snapping off. This PR fixes this issue, while simplifying color/activation highlighting for UI elements.

Add scrolling ability to UI.Text. | This is a breaking change for the C api's ui_text.This also fixes the height of UI.Text, which previously was maybe half a line too tall! This may result in small changes in layout height, and can be particularly noticeable if you were previously vertically centering your text, especially with single line text content.
Fix soft keyboard lingering key release. | This fixes an issue where buttons that close the soft keyboard don't get released until the keyboard is opened again.

Fix simulator window not exactly restoring its position when maximized. |

Fix omitEmptyFrames with hands | This fixes an issue where hand meshes alone would not keep the primary composition layer alive during omitEmptyFrames. Since hands use predicted time, they were getting drawn _after_ the composition layer checked for draw items.This change moves that hand draw code before the visibility check, so hand meshes are now accounted for properly.

Text layout refactor | Revision of #1093, use notes from here. This PR backs away from switching to ascenders for layout height, and returns to cap height, since layout ascenders can be hugely varied from font to font.This reworks how some details of font rendering are implements. Results should be more pixel accurate, and a few more tools are provided for working with text. Impact to existing UI was minimized, and most existing layouts shouldn't change. Notably, the space between multi-line tex

Default theme aliasing. | Reduces aliasing a bit at glancing angles in the default theme. Button chamfers were acting as an extra-visible fast transition between dark and light areas of the mesh, and this variation was adding too much variation for 4x MSAA to blend out well.

Improve edges of the UI Box with Transparency.MSAA | This removes jaggies from the UI box's harsh `discard`. It takes advantage of the recent Transparency.MSAA mode to do transparent AA without zbuffer artifacts.This also uses the more recent "touch" visual effect, though slightly modified to work a bit better with alpha to coverage transparency. This looks a bit different on Android vs PC due to platform specific alpha to coverage dithering implementation differences. This may be a tweakable de

Fix grab bar size/positioning, and update to some newer UI features. | Switched placement over to UISettings based values. This lines up better with the UI in a wider variety of cases.![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7b3e3505-3d49-4ff4-aa13-7ce0c35c398c)

Fix multiview double draw | Multiview will try to draw twice regardless of whether or not it's drawing to a multiview surface. This results in weird behavior (doubled object visuals) when drawing to rendertargets with only a single surface!Ideally, finding a way to turn off multiview for mono targets would be best, but realistically, it looks like shader variations may be necessary to support this scenario in GLES. That's not a small project, so this is a temporary fix that should eliminate most

Fix render model ext interaction with the CameraRoot matrix. | When Renderer.CameraRoot was not Identity, the render model extension was displaying models in the wrong place! This fixed it so the ext properly accounts for the CameraRoot transform.
A slightly higher texture for the hand mesh | Pushes the opaque part up the fingers of the hand material a bit, knuckles sometimes look a bit weird opaque with the finger tubes.

Fix a few issues with tests | Depth was flipped due to a mistake in TestRenderProps.TestInteractorPoints wasn't stepping properly due to a missed line.TestAudio was modifying clip planes without restoring them when finished.

Renderloop dirty GL | This fixes an issue where not dirtying the `<canvas>` WebGL context would cause the frame to not be submitted to the AR/VR device (especially magic leap). In some cases this is not tolerated well -- i.e. if you do not end a frame that you started the native loop will fail to render anymore.- This changes it so that any time you have a dirty, visible context, it will go through the swapchain.- Additionally, the Magic Leap case to always be considered dirty for swap purposes.
Fix HTMLTemplateElement typo | Fixes https://github.com/webmixedreality/exokit/issues/501.This gets `networked-aframe` working.

Reality tabs: far depth | Reality tabs are currently initialized with a depth texture of zeroes. This means the new tab wins the depth test before any rendering has started. Therefore if it misses the first frame (very likely), it will cause a display wipe until the tab starts to render.To the user this appears as a black blink glitch.This change adds a depth clear to render target context creation so that reality tabs start with far depth, avoiding an overwrite before the tabs' rendering has st
Use display aspect ratio in 2D browser layers XR blend | Fixes https://github.com/webmixedreality/exokit/issues/667.

3D favicon transparency | The launcher favicon in https://github.com/webmixedreality/exokit/pull/683 has a pure black base, which is hard to see on AR devices in scenarios without a background.This colors the icon with a lighter `#333` to fix the problem.

AR floor origin | Fixes #391.Fixes #506.WebXR specifies that the origin of the XR coordinate frame should be the floor. This is not currently true (on Magic Leap), resulting in the sky content problem, which many people just hack around by moving content down.This is suboptimal not only because it requires content to change based on AR user agent, but it also means that content written for VR sometimes needs tweaks for AR, which it shouldn't.This PR uses the AR render loop to detect the floor an

Reality tabs keyboard keys rendering | Fixes the reality tabs in-world keyboard enter key edge case: alpha testing and cache canvas size being too small to compute the key mesh image.![ml_20190112_16 05 44](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6926057/51078516-0cef1500-1684-11e9-9dea-b34bbc092286.jpg)

Tutorial page axis helper | Makes the reality tabs initial tutorial HTML page use an axis helper like in 3D engines.The floor mesh that used to be there is made smaller (1m), because previously it was huge (20m) and clipped outside of most IRL spaces, especially when meshing.

Make fake vr display stereo again | This makes the VR/XR device emulation render in stereo by default.We had this before, then switched to mono emulation. However, too many experiences assume that each eye is half of the screen and ignore the actual matrices coming in, which makes canvas size off by a factor of two.Stereo rendering makes for a less clean user experience but a more accurate XR emulation one, while functionally fixing experiences that have headset-like assumptions about the VR/XR

Consider blitFramebuffer as a dirtying WebGL operation | This fixes the corner case when the only operation in the render loop is a framebuffer blit.It's more common on XR when generating eye textures.

Real devicePixelRatio | For a long time we've faked the `window. devicePixelRatio` property. This PR actually queries for it, while allowing Retina (scaling) mode on MacOS to function normally, by adding another framebuffer stretch blit phase to the 2D rendering case.This allows us to get antialiasing to the main framebuffer, which improves the quality of rendering in the 2D (non-HMD) case, including WebXR emulation.Fixes https://github.com/exokitxr/exokit/issues/982.

Oculus Touch controller naming | Fixes handed `Oculus Touch` controller ids. Also, fixes the missing fake vr display gamepad id.Fixes Supercraft glitching.

Bugfix OpenVR eye offset tracking | Both eyes were pulling offset data from the left eye for the OpenVR case.This only affected legacy WebVR applications using `THREE.VREffect`, but made everything look incorrect in OpenVR. An example of this is A-Blast.
